texto: Much of the time the mind is wandering, either drawn to focus, ruminate, or push away unpleasant experiences, or chasing after stuff we like. But if we don’t practise being still, we are prone to get blown about by every wind, buffeted by the ups and downs of life. By training to pay attention precisely and gently to the breath, coming back again and again, we cultivate a resilience that allows us to be present when difficulty and temptation arises. Distractions still come, but we don’t get so lost in them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ed Halliwell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Much of the time the mind is wandering, either drawn to focus, ruminate, or push away unpleasant …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, breath, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e vida.

frase_original: Much of the time the mind is wandering, either drawn to focus, ruminate, or push away unpleasant experiences, or chasing after stuff we like. But if we don’t practise being still, we are prone to get blown about by every wind, buffeted by the ups and downs of life. By training to pay attention precisely and gently to the breath, coming back again and again, we cultivate a resilience that allows us to be present when difficulty and temptation arises. Distractions still come, but we don’t get so lost in them.

texto: ...there is something very vital happening when we breathe—without it we die—but trying to speed it up, force it, grasp it, push it away or control it tends to get in the way. As in breathing, so in life—we can learn a lot from the natural rhythm, pace, and un-fussiness of the way breath continues its work, without making a big deal out of it.
explicacao: Citação de Ed Halliwell sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “...there is something very vital happening when we breathe—without it we die—but trying to speed …” articula reflexao, breath, vida em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e trabalho.

As in breathing, so in life—we can learn a lot from the natural rhythm, pace, and un-fussiness of the way breath continues its work, without making a big deal out of it.
